actions.title:
Universal Program:
MTSS Academics
TK-12
actions.description:
SVUSD will provide all students with high-quality, universal instruction as
part of the Multi-Tiered System of Supports (MTSS) for academics. This
foundational level of instruction ensures that every student receives
effective, evidence-based teaching practices in their regular classroom
setting. It emphasizes inclusivity and aims to meet the diverse needs of all
students from first instruction, reducing the need for future interventions.
In order to have an effective MTSS Academics, we will provide a robust
professional development program to improve our universal classroom
instructional practices and classroom intervention strategies along with a
comprehensive intervention system.
Our MTSS Academics program will contribute to improvement in many of
our student outcomes, especially M2.1 ELA and M2.5 Math.
Action 2.1 is a description of our overall program. See Actions 2.2
($1,409,930.00), 2.6 ($0), and 2.8 ($113,071.00) for highlighted
information and related costs. Most Actions in Goal 2 are components of
MTSS Academics.

actions.title:
Intervention System:
Elementary Reading
Intervention Program
actions.description:
We will provide targeted support to elementary students who need
additional help in reading through the Elementary Reading Intervention
P r o gram as part of our MTSS Academics. This program employs trained
reading interventionists who work closely with students to improve their
foundational reading skills, comprehension, and overall literacy.
Interventionists use data to tailor to each student's specific needs.
The reading interventionists are trained and supported by the MTSS TOSA
for ELA (See Action 2.20) to ensure that reading intervention is
implemented consistently, rigorously, and is evidence-based across the
district.
A strong reading intervention program is available for all students, with a
particular focus on our foster youth, English learners, and low-income
students.
Our Elementary Reading Intervention program will contribute to improving
our student outcomes in metrics M2.1 ELA and M2.2 i-Ready ELA.
This action addresses the requirements for LREBG. For 2025-26, LREBG
provides $1,409,930 to fund this action. Research shows that targeting
response to interventions can be effective with an effect size of 1.29
(Hattie, 2018).

actions.title:
Intervention System:
Additional Resources
for Reading
Intervention Program
actions.description:
We will provide supplemental resources, including multi-sensory
manipulatives, to enrich our elementary reading intervention program. This
will help students learn through touch, sight, and sound. The supplemental
r e s ources are crucial for supporting students who benefit from engaging
with materials in various ways, ensuring all learners can grasp reading
skills more effectively.
A strong reading intervention program is available for all students, with a
particular focus on our foster youth, English learners, and low-income
students.
An appropriately resourced Elementary Reading Intervention program will
contribute to improving our student outcomes in metrics M2.1 ELA and 2.2
i-Ready ELA.

actions.title:
Intervention System:
Elementary Extended
Learning Time
actions.description:
We will expand learning opportunities for elementary students by
supporting schools with the Extended Learning Time program as part of
MTSS Academics. This program extends the instructional day to include
before or after school learning time, providing additional time for academic
support and personalized learning experiences and is based on school
data.
Extended learning time is available to all students, but is especially helpful
for our foster youth, English learners, and low-income students who may
benefit from more time to learn.
Our Extended Learning Time program will contribute to improving our
student outcomes in metrics M2.2 i-Ready ELA. and M2.6 Math K-2, M2.7
Math 3-6.
As each school have different needs, each school is able to use a part of
their school site allocation to provide Extended Learning Time. Cost
($145,964.00) is accounted for using Goal 3 Action 3.6, LCAP school site
allocation, and is not included here.

actions.title:
Intervention System:
Elementary i-Ready
Personalized
Pathways
actions.description:
We will provide individualized intervention through the Elementary i-Ready
Personalized Pathways program. This program utilizes the i-Ready online
platform to deliver customized instructional content that adapts to each
student's learning level and pace.
The i-Ready Personalized Pathways is available to all students, with a
particular focus on foster youth, English learners, and low-income
students.
The program will contribute to improving our student outcomes in metric
M2.2 i-Ready ELA.
Funding for i-Ready ($58,660.20) is accounted for in Goal 3 Action 3.10,
technology licenses and subscriptions, and is not included here.

actions.title:
Intervention System:
Secondary Extended
Learning Time
actions.description:
We will provide educational opportunities for secondary students through
the Secondary Extended Learning Time program, which will extend the
school day to provide extra time for academic enrichment, homework
assistance, and skill development. It targets students who benefit from
additional instructional time to master complex subjects or complete
coursework and is based on school data.
Extended learning time is available for all students, with a particular focus
on foster youth, English learners, and low-income students who may
benefit from additional time to learn.
Our secondary Extended Learning Time will contribute to improving our
student outcomes in metrics M2.3 SBAC ELA, M2.4 IXL ELA, M2.9 SBAC
Math, and M2.8 IXL Math.
As each school have different needs, each school is able to use a part of
their school site allocation to provide Extended Learning Time. Cost
($145,964.00) is accounted for using Goal 3 Action 3.6, LCAP school site
allocation, and is not included here.

actions.title:
Intervention System:
ELD Instructional
Aides (IAs) to
Support Multilingual
Learners
actions.description:
ELD instructional aides (IAs) will support multilingual learners, enhancing
their academic success in the classroom. The IAs are trained and
equipped with the skills and resources needed to assist students who are
learning English as an additional language, providing both linguistic and
academic support. By working directly with multilingual learners, the IAs
help bridge communication gaps, facilitate better understanding of the
curriculum, and tailor instructional methods to meet diverse language
learning needs.
ELD IAs will contribute to improving our student outcomes in metrics M2.10
ELPI and 2.11 Reclassification.

actions.title:
Intervention System:
Comprehensive
Assessment System
TK-12
actions.description:
To make sure that our interventions are data-based and targeted to meet
students’ needs, we will maintain an effective infrastructure to support a
comprehensive assessment system that is the core of our interventions.
The comprehensive assessment system will provide holistic information
about our students academics as well as social-emotional and behavior
well-being.
A comprehensive assessment system benefits all of our learners, but is
crucial for making sure that we are providing targeted assistance to
remove barriers to learning for our foster youth, English learners, and low-
income students.
A comprehensive assessment system will contribute to many metrics,
especially M2.1 ELA and M2.5 Math.
Please see Actions 2.9, 2.10, 2.11, 2.12, 2.13, 2.14, and 2.15 for more
information and related metrics.

actions.title:
Intervention System:
Elementary
Academics
Assessment and
Progress Monitoring
actions.description:
We will have an elementary comprehensive assessment and progress
monitoring system through the use of educational platforms to ensure a
successful MTSS Academics that supports all students’ needs.
T h e assessment system includes universal screeners, diagnostics, and
progress monitoring of instructional and intervention progress. By regularly
assessing students' academic progress through these tools, educators can
obtain valuable insights into individual and group performance, identify
learning gaps, and tailor instruction to meet the specific needs of each
student.
The assessment system benefits all of our learners, but is crucial for
making sure that we are providing targeted assistance to remove barriers
to learning for our foster youth, English learners, and low-income students.
Currently, we use i-Ready and eduCLIMBER as part of our MTSS
Academics comprehensive assessment and progress monitoring system.
They will contribute to our student outcomes in many metrics, especially
M2.2 i-Ready ELA, M2.6 Math K-2, and M2.7 Math 3-6.
Cost for i-Ready ($58,660.20) and eduCLIMBER ($296,484.92) is
accounted for in Goal 3 Action 3.10, technology licenses and subscriptions,
and is not included here.

actions.title:
Intervention System:
Secondary
Academics
Assessment and
Progress Monitoring
actions.description:
This action is REVISED for 2025-26. We will DISCONTINUE the use of IXL
and will CONTINUE with the use of eduCLIMBER as part of our secondary
assessment system and will explore using other assessments.
W e will have a secondary comprehensive assessment and progress
monitoring system through the use of educational platforms to ensure a
successful MTSS Academics that supports all students’ needs.
The assessment system includes universal screeners, diagnostics, and
progress monitoring of instructional and intervention progress. By regularly
assessing students' academic progress through these tools, educators can
obtain valuable insights into individual and group performance, identify
learning gaps, and tailor instruction to meet the specific needs of each
student.
This will benefit all of our learners, but is crucial for making sure that we
are providing targeted assistance to remove barriers to learning for our
foster youth, English learners, and low-income students.
Currently, we use eduCLIMBER as part of our MTSS Academics
comprehensive assessment and progress monitoring system for
secondary. eduCLIMBER ($296,484.92) is accounted for in Goal 3 Action
3.10, technology licenses and subscriptions, and is not included here.
They will contribute to our student outcomes in many metrics, especially
M2.3 SBAC ELA and M2.9 SBAC Math.

actions.title:
Intervention System:
Secondary
Intervention
Participation
Progress Monitoring
System
actions.description:
We will implement an intervention fidelity and progress monitoring system
for secondary intervention. This system uses an online platform to ensure
that students attend their interventions and enrichment opportunities and
that data on their participation and progress are accurately recorded. The
progress monitoring ensures that students will receive personalized
learning opportunities and targeted support based on their unique needs.
Currently, the online platform is Flex Time.
Real-time data and feedback on student participation and progress is
important for everyone, especially foster youth, English learners, and low-
income students to ensure they receive the support they need in a timely
manner.
This will contribute to improving our student outcomes in metrics M2.3
SBAC ELA, M2.4 IXL ELA, M2.8 IXL Math, and M2.9 SBAC Math.
Cost ($30,000.00) is accounted for in Goal 3 Action 3.10 and is not
included here.

actions.title:
Intervention System:
Language
Assessment
actions.description:
We will continue to provide language assessment for our multilingual
learners. The Language Assessment Center will initially evaluate the
English language proficiency of students using the initial ELPAC (English
Language Proficiency Assessments for California) while the Coordinator of
Assessment/IDT will administer the annual summative ELPAC. The initial
ELPAC plays a vital role in understanding new students' English language
skills as soon as they join our schools, while the summative ELPAC helps
us monitor students’ progress from year to year.
Language assessments give us the information we need to provide
language support for our multilingual learners.
Language assessments will contribute to our student outcomes in metrics
2.10 ELPI and M2.11 Reclassification.

actions.title:
Intervention System:
English Language
Assessment and
Progress Monitoring
using Ellevation
actions.description:
Our comprehensive assessment and progress monitoring system will also
include the Ellevation platform to assess and monitor the language
development progress of our English Learners. This will help make sure
that our language instruction and interventions for English learners are
data-driven and targeted so that students can reclassify in a timely manner
as well as achieve success in all curricular areas.
The use of a progress monitoring system for English learners will
contribute to our student outcomes in metrics 2.10 ELPI and M2.11
Reclassification.

actions.title:
Intervention System:
ML Site Coordinators
actions.description:
We will continue to provide multilingual learner (ML) site coordinators at
e a c h school and provide targeted professional development to build their
expertise. The ML site coordinators monitor the progress of our multilingual
learners and make instructional recommendations to the school. The ML
site coordinators closely watch how students are progressing with English
language acquisition, using our assessment system to understand each
student’s needs. They use this information to suggest the best ways to
help each student develop English language proficiency.
The work that ML Site Coordinators do will contribute to our student
outcomes in metrics 2.10 ELPI and M2.11 Reclassification.

actions.title:
Intervention System:
Student Study Team
actions.description:
Our Student Study Team (SST) helps us understand and meet the
i n d ividual needs of each student through a detailed, personalized plan,
developed through the SST Process or the 504 process. We bring together
teachers, parents/guardians, and, if needed, specialists to plan about how
best to support each student's learning and growth at school.
The Student Study Team is an important component of our intervention as
part of MTSS Academics and MTSS SEB (See Action 1.6). The SST
supports our students who need more personalized help, which is
particularly important for our foster youth, English learners, and low-income
students.
Our Student Study Team will contribute to improvement in our student
outcomes as measured by many metrics, including M2.1 ELA and M2.5
Math.
See Goal 1 Action 1.6 for more information about how SST supports
social-emotional, behavioral well-being and related cost. Cost ($72,660.00)
is accounted for in contributing action Goal 1 Action 1.6.

actions.title:
Robust Professional
Development:
Elementary
Instructional Coaches
actions.description:
This Action is DISCONTINUED starting 2025-26. Please see Action 2.17
and 2.20 for updates.
T o support the MTSS Academics, our elementary instructional coaches will
provide professional development on ELA and Math for the SV Reads and
SV Counts initiatives. SV Reads include literacy best practices,
foundational literacy skills, and instructional strategies for all students while
SV Counts includes a focus on developing conceptual understanding,
meaningful student discourse, and the development of number sense.
The goal is to meet the diverse needs of all students from the very
beginning, reducing the need for additional interventions. A critical part of
their work will be to provide professional development on classroom
intervention strategies for English Language Arts and Math. Both reducing
the need for interventions and classroom intervention strategies are
particularly important for our foster youth, English learners, and low-income
students, as they help every student get the classroom support necessary
to achieve their full academic potential as soon as possible.
The coaches’ work will include facilitating Professional Learning
Community (PLC) meetings, site-based and district-level professional
development, one-on-one coaching, and team coaching.
Our elementary instructional coaches will contribute to improvement in our
student outcomes in M2.2 i-Ready ELA, M2.6 Math K-2, and M2.7 Math 3-
6.

actions.title:
Robust Professional
Development:
Elementary MTSS
TOSA at Title-1
Schools
actions.description:
This action has been revised for 2025-26. Instead of having a full-time
instructional coach at each Title-I school site, we will provide additional
days of TOSA support at Title-I schools in conjunction with Action 2.20.
To further enhance our support for our low-income students, we will make
sure that our Title-1 school sites have more support from the MTSS TOSA
since our Title-1 school sites have a higher percentage of low-income
students and our data shows that they have higher academic needs.
The TOSA's work will include facilitating Professional Learning Community
(PLC) meetings as detailed in Action 2.20, and will provide additional days
of one-on-one coaching, and team coaching with a focus on instructional
best practices and classroom intervention strategies.
Our elementary MTSS TOSA will contribute to improvement in our student
outcomes in M2.2 i-Ready ELA, M2.6 Math K-2, and M2.7 Math 3-6.

actions.title:
Robust Professional
Development:
Secondary
Instructional Coaches
actions.description:
This Action is DISCONTINUED starting 2025-26. Please see Action 2.21
for updates.
T o support the MTSS Academics, our secondary instructional coaches will
provide professional development on research-based classroom
strategies, including grading for equity, integrated ELD, comprehensible
input for language support, cross content literacy, equitable and engaging
math instruction.
The goal is to meet the diverse needs of all students from the very
beginning, reducing the need for additional interventions. A critical part of
their work will be to provide professional development on classroom
intervention strategies for all curricular areas with a focus on SVUSD
signature practices for instruction and intervention.
Both reducing the need for interventions and classroom intervention
strategies are particularly important for our foster youth, English learners,
and low-income students as they help every student get the classroom
support necessary to achieve their full academic potential as soon as
possible.
The coaches’ work will include facilitating Professional Learning
Community (PLC) meetings, site-based and district-level professional
development, collaborative principal/coach instructional focus, one-on-one
coaching, and team coaching.
Our secondary instructional coaches will contribute to improvement in our
student outcomes in M2.3 SBAC ELA, M2.4 IXL ELA, M2.8 IXL Math, and
M2.9 SBAC Math.

actions.title:
Robust Professional
Development:
Elementary MTSS
TOSA for Academics
actions.description:
This action has been revised for 2025-26.
The district provides a Teacher on Special Assignment (TOSA) to support
P r o fessional Learning Communities (PLCs) through the Multi-Tiered
System of Supports (MTSS). The TOSA helps teachers work together to
review student progress, share effective teaching strategies, and plan
lessons that meet the needs of all learners. By focusing on early support
and intervention, teachers can better assist students who need extra help,
with a particular focus on English learners, foster youth, and low-income
students. With this guidance, schools create a more supportive learning
environment where every student has the opportunity to succeed.
The elementary MTSS TOSAs for Academics will contribute to
improvement in our student outcomes in metrics M2.2 i-Ready ELA, M2.6
Math K-2, and M2.7 Math 3-6.

actions.title:
Robust Professional
Development:
Secondary MTSS
TOSA for Academics
actions.description:
This action has been revised for 2025-26.
The district provides a Teacher on Special Assignment (TOSA) to support
c u r riculum development and implementation through the Multi-Tiered
System of Supports (MTSS), to ensure a guaranteed and viable curriculum
for all students. The TOSA helps teachers develop standards aligned
lessons and assessments and work together to review student progress,
share effective teaching strategies, and implement interventions lessons
that meet the needs of all learners. By focusing on curricular alignment and
intervention, teachers can better use data to identify students who need
extra help, with a particular focus on English learners, foster youth, and
low-income students. With this guidance, schools create a more equitable
and supportive learning environment where every student has the
opportunity to succeed.
The secondary MTSS TOSAs for Academics will contribute to
improvement in our student outcomes in metrics M2.3 SBAC ELA, M2.4
IXL ELA.

actions.title:
Robust Professional
Development: TOSA
for English Language
Development (ELD)
actions.description:
The Teacher on Special Assignment for English Language Development
(ELD TOSA) will continue to provide district-wide professional development
and support for our Designated English Language Development program
a n d integrated ELD strategies. The TOSA provides robust professional
development focused on strengthening the expertise of teachers who work
with multilingual learners.
The training includes best practices for language instruction and strategies
to support language acquisition. This will equip teachers to meet the
unique needs of multilingual learners, helping them to succeed in
mastering both the English language and their academic subjects.
The ELD TOSA will contribute to our student outcomes in metrics M2.10
ELPI and M2.11 Reclassification Rate.

actions.title:
Robust Professional
Development:
Integrated English
Language
Development
actions.description:
We will provide professional development (PD) opportunities for integrated
English language development, with a focus on strategies to support Long-
term English Learners (LTEL).
While many of the PD will be facilitated by our TOSAs in-person, we will
also utilize the Ellevation Strategies platform. This provides our teachers
with additional online asynchronous learning opportunities, allowing them
to enhance their skills in integrated English language development at their
own pace. The training focuses on effective strategies for integrating
language development into everyday classroom activities across all
curricular areas.
The in-person and online PD will contribute to our student outcomes in
metrics M2.10 ELPI and M2.11 Reclassification Rate.

actions.title:
Robust Professional
Development: CRLP
for Language
Development
actions.description:
We will continue to provide an on-going, professional development
program through the California Reading and Literature Project (CRLP) to
support language development for our multilingual learners. Our
collaboration with CRLP provides our educators with extensive training and
resources focused on effective integrated ELD teaching strategies. By
participating in the CRLP, teachers gain valuable insights and tools that
improve student engagement and language development.
CRLP professional development will contribute to our student outcomes in
metrics M2.10 ELPI and M2.11 Reclassification Rate.
Cost is accounted for using other funding sources and is not included here.

actions.title:
Robust Professional
Development:
Effective Dual
Language Immersion
TK-12
actions.description:
We will provide on-going, professional development (PD) on effective
language development instruction for our dual language immersion (DLI)
program. Through the PD, teachers gain valuable insights and tools that
improve student engagement and language development.
DLI professional development will contribute to our student outcomes in
metrics M2.10 ELPI and M2.11 Reclassification Rate.
Cost is accounted for using other funding sources and is not included here.

actions.title:
Robust Professional
Development:
Special Education
Coach
actions.description:
Special Education Coach will continue to provide professional development
focused on inclusive practices. The coach will work with teachers across
the district to enhance their understanding and implementation of
strategies that support all students, especially our students with disabilities.
The coach will provide district-wide professional development, facilitate
team learning, and one-on-one coaching.
The work of our Special Education Coach will contribute to our student
outcomes in many metrics, including M2.1 ELA and M2.5 Math for students
with disabilities.
Cost is accounted for using other funding sources and is not included here.

actions.title:
Targeted Program:
Language Acquisition
Department
actions.description:
We will maintain an effective infrastructure to support the Language
Acquisition program which provides opportunities, instructional programs,
assessments, family engagement and other structures. Language
Acquisition supports multilingual learner progress towards English
language development and ensures that all of our support for multilingual
learners are comprehensive, strategic, and evidence-based. The
department includes the director and secretary.
The work that they do will contribute to our student outcomes in metrics
M2.10 ELPI and M2.11 Reclassification Rate.

actions.title:
Targeted Program:
Elementary
Designated English
Language
Development (ELD)
actions.description:
All elementary multilingual learners will be provided at least 30 minutes of
daily Designated English Language Development (Designated ELD). To do
so, we will continue to provide designated ELD time, specifically designed
to support students who are learning English. Designated ELD offers
f o cused and intensive lessons that supports English language acquisition.
Designated ELD will contribute to our student outcomes in metrics M2.10
ELPI and M2.11 Reclassification Rate.
This is a statement of intent. There is no extra cost associated with this
action as this is a regular part of elementary classrooms with ML students
enrolled and is based on the CA and district EL Roadmap. General funds
pay for teacher salaries.

actions.title:
Targeted Program:
Secondary
Newcomer Courses
actions.description:
We will offer Designated ELD, intensive, focused courses in English
Language Arts/English Language Development (ELA/ELD) specifically
d e signed for newcomer students. The courses aim to provide rapid English
language acquisition.
Newcomer DELD courses will contribute to our student outcomes in metric
M2.10 ELPI.

actions.title:
Targeted Program:
Secondary Intensive
LTEL Courses
actions.description:
We will offer designated English Language Development (ELD) courses for
Long-Term English Learners (LTELs) at the secondary level, focusing on
intensive reading and language arts. The courses are designed to deepen
language skills and enhance literacy through targeted, skill-specific
instruction. We will also develop and offer additional designated ELD
courses for LTELs focusing on language development in other curricular
areas.
LTEL courses will contribute to our student outcomes in metrics M2.10
ELPI and M2.11 Reclassification Rate.

actions.title:
Targeted Program:
ML Site Based
Allocations
actions.description:
We will allocate funds to each school site, allowing schools to directly
support their multilingual learners (ML) based on individual school data.
This tailored funding approach enables each school to implement
strategies and resources that best meet the unique needs of the school’s
multilingual learners. For example, a school might use these funds for
additional professional development on integrated ELD and culturally
proficient language development strategies, providing additional hours of
ELD instructional aide support, and/or providing additional parent
engagement activities such as informational workshops.
Action 2.31 is part of our strategy to support schools that received the
lowest performance level on one or more state indicators on the 2023
Dashboard for foster youth, English learners, and/or low-income students.
Site-based ML allocations will contribute to our student outcomes in
metrics M2.10 ELPI and M2.11 Reclassification Rate.

actions.title:
Targeted Program:
Dual Language
Immersion Program
TK-12
actions.description:
We will provide additional support to the TK-12 Dual Language Immersion
(DLI) program. DLI provides students with the opportunity to become fluent
in two languages. This program immerses students in both English and
Spanish, offering additional support to ensure all students achieve high
linguistic and academic proficiency.
DLI will contribute to our student outcomes in metrics M2.10 ELPI and
M2.11 Reclassification Rate. DLI will also contribute to our student
outcomes in metric M2.18 Biliteracy.
Cost is accounted for using other funds. This action is funded using
Educator Effectiveness Fund (EEF), $4,200.

actions.title:
Targeted Program:
Elementary
Newcomer Summer
Camp
actions.description:
We will have a newcomer summer camp, designed specifically to support
students who are new to the United States. This summer camp helps these
students improve their English language skills and get acquainted with the
American school system before the regular academic year begins. The
summer camp ensures that newcomers are welcomed, well-prepared and
confident to start their school journey successfully.
The newcomer summer camp will contribute to our student outcomes in
metric M2.10 ELPI.
This action is funded with Title-III, $150,000.

actions.title:
Targeted Program:
Secondary ML
Summer School
Support
actions.description:
We will offer multilingual learner (ML) support for secondary summer
school students. This will provide additional opportunities to enhance
English language development during the summer break. In addition, we
will provide language support so that MLs can be successful with summer
credit recovery and electives courses, helping them to graduate in a timely
manner. The support includes intensive instruction in English language
arts, professional development to teachers on integrated ELD, and ELD
instructional aides. We will begin implementing ML summer school support
in the summer of 2025.
The secondary ML summer school support will contribute to our outcomes
in metrics M2.10 ELPI and M2.11 Reclassification Rate.
The ML summer support is in the planning stage in 2024-25. The current
estimate is $3,000 for PD on integrated ELD strategies for secondary
summer school teachers using Title-III funds.

actions.title:
Targeted Program:
Multilingual Learner
Early Childhood
Program
actions.description:
We will provide additional support for the multilingual learner (ML) early
childhood program, known as Learning Links, aimed at supporting young
children develop English language proficiency. The program focuses on
early language development, providing a strong foundation in both English
and the child's home language. By offering specialized support and
resources from an early age, we ensure that multilingual learners are well
prepared and confident as they progress into their elementary school
years.
The ML early childhood program will contribute to our student outcomes in
metric M2.10 ELPI.

actions.title:
Targeted Program:
Elementary MTSS
Academics
Department
actions.description:
We will maintain a focused infrastructure to support MTSS Academics in
order to guarantee the overall effectiveness of the MTSS Academics for all
students. As part of MTSS Academics, the elementary department will plan
and implement culturally proficient district-wide professional development,
instructional programs, and evidence-based interventions that are data-
driven and targeted, with a special focus on removing barriers to learning
for our foster youth, English learners, and low-income students.
Their work ensures that first instruction is effective, reducing the need for
future interventions and that interventions, once needed, are appropriately
tailored and supportive for students who may require additional resources
to achieve academic success.
The work that they do will contribute to improving our student outcomes in
many metrics, especially M2.2 i-Ready ELA, M2.6 Math K-2, and M2.7
Math 3-6.

actions.title:
Targeted Program:
Secondary MTSS
Academics
Department
actions.description:
We will maintain a focused infrastructure to support MTSS Academics in
order to guarantee the overall effectiveness of the MTSS Academics for all
students. As part of MTSS Academics, the secondary department will plan
and implement culturally proficient district-wide professional development,
instructional programs, and evidence-based interventions that are data-
driven and targeted, with a special focus on removing barriers to learning
for our foster youth, English learners, and low-income students.
Their work ensures that first instruction is effective, reducing the need for
future interventions and that interventions, once needed, are appropriately
tailored and supportive for students who may require additional resources
to achieve academic success.
This will contribute to improving our student outcomes in many metrics,
especially M2.3 SBAC ELA, M2.4 IXL ELA, M2.8 IXL Math, and M2.9
SBAC Math.

actions.title:
Target Program:
Personalized
Instruction Through
Instruction Design
and Technology
actions.description:
We will maintain a focused infrastructure for instructional design and
technology, with the goal of ensuring standards-based, personalized
instruction for our different learners. We will empower teachers to use
timely student assessment data to make instructional decisions, integrate
technology into their lessons, and use inclusive practices to support all
students' diverse needs.
This is good for all students, but it is especially important to personalize
instruction for our foster youth, English learners, and low-income students
who face additional barriers to learning.
Personalized instruction will contribute to our outcomes in metrics M2.1
ELA and M2.5 Math.

actions.title:
Targeted Program:
Magnet Schools K-12
actions.description:
We will provide additional support for Magnet Schools K-12, enhancing the
u n iq ue educational programs these schools offer across various
disciplines, from science and technology to arts and languages. This
support includes supplemental funding for specialized materials, training
for teachers, and resources that enhance the distinctive curricula of each
magnet program. By investing in these specialized educational
environments, we ensure that students have access to high-quality,
diverse learning opportunities that prepare them for future academic and
career success.
The magnet school programs will contribute to our student outcomes in
metrics M2.1 ELA and M2.5 Math.
Cost is accounted for using other funding sources and is not included here.

actions.title:
Targeted Program:
Gifted and Talented
Program (GATE)
actions.description:
We will provide additional support for the Gifted and Talented Education
(GATE) program, ensuring that GATE testing processes and instructional
practices are fair and inclusive of all of our students.
This includes making sure that our identification criteria and methods are
able to identify and nurture the unique abilities of all students, with a
particular focus on foster youth, English learners, and low-income
students. By providing additional resources and tailored support, we aim to
foster the intellectual and creative potential of diverse gifted students,
helping them thrive in challenging and stimulating learning environments.
The GATE program will contribute to our student outcomes in metric M2.20
Gifted Identification.

actions.title:
Targeted Program:
Advanced Placement
(AP) and
International
Baccalaureate (IB)
Program
actions.description:
We will provide support for Advanced Placement (AP) and International
Baccalaureate (IB) programs to becoming culturally proficient college-
preparatory learning opportunities for students. This additional support
includes funding for more course offerings, culturally proficient resources,
and training for teachers on culturally proficient and inclusive practices to
support all students, with a particular focus on foster youth, English
learners, and low-income students.
AP and IB programs will contribute to our student outcomes in metrics
M2.16 AP.

actions.title:
Targeted Program:
Elementary
Combination Class
Reduction
actions.description:
This action has been revised for 2025-26. Instead of reducing combination
classes at Title-1 sites, we will reduce combination classes for
Kindergarten/first grade combination classes at all elementary schools.
We will provide additional teacher positions so that there are no
combination classes in Grades K/1 in order to improve our student to
teacher ratio as part of our learning recovery efforts.
This action will be paid out of one-time money which is expected to expire.
Eliminating combination classes will contribute to our student outcomes in
metric M2.2 i-Ready ELA, M2.6 Math K-2, and M2.7 Math 3-6 for Grades
K-1
This action addresses the requirements for LREBG. For 2025-26, LREBG
provides $545,954 to fund this action. The Hun School of Princeton (2019)
indicates the significance of student-teacher ratios in educational settings.

actions.title:
Targeted Program:
College/Career
Readiness
Department
actions.description:
We will continue to maintain an effective infrastructure to support
College/Career Readiness to ensure students from kindergarten through
12th grade get the support they need for future success in college and
careers. This includes programs like AVID (Advancement Via Individual
Determination) and CTE (Career Technical Education), which help
students develop skills and interests in various professional fields. By
providing these resources, we aim to prepare every student for the
opportunities that lie ahead, with a particular focus on foster youth, English
learners, and low-income students.
College/Career Readiness will contribute to our student outcomes in metric
M2.12 College/Career.

actions.title:
Targeted Program:
Integrated
Professional
Development for
SAI/SPED
actions.description:
We will ensure that our special education staff are able to join in the
professional development provided to all staff, including during PLC,
district provided professional development, and/or school provided
professional development. We will do so by providing supplemental
funding for release time and substitute coverage. This way, we can make
sure that all staff receive the same knowledge and skills they need to
support our students.
This will contribute to our student outcomes in many metrics, especially
M2.1 ELA and M2.5 Math for students with disabilities.
This action addresses the requirements for LREBG. For 2025-26, LREBG
provides $84,770 to fund this action. Research shows that general
education and special education teachers collaborating in PLC has a
significant impact on student outcomes for SWD (Priziellie, Schmidt, and
Spiller, 2016).

actions.title:
Robust Professional
Development:
Professional
Learning Community
(PLC)
actions.description:
We will strengthen Professional Learning Communities (PLCs) across all
grade levels to improve teaching and student learning. In PLCs, teachers
collaborate regularly to review student progress, share effective strategies,
and adjust instruction to meet individual needs. The district provides
training, resources, and dedicated time to support high-quality teaching
practices. This ongoing collaboration fosters a culture of continuous
improvement, ensuring that all students receive the best possible
education.
PLCs will contribute to improvement in our student outcomes in M2.1 ELA
and M2.5 Math.
There is no cost associated with this action for secondary as it is a regular
part of the schools' schedules. For elementary, cost ($1,750,248.00) is
accounted for in action Goal 2 Action 2.46, in order to release teachers
from classroom duties to participate.

actions.title:
Robust Professional
Development:
Elementary PLC
Support Teams
actions.description:
To improve ELA and Math outcomes for foster youth, English learners, and
low-income students, Professional Learning Community (PLC) support
teams will release teachers to attend PLCs to collaborate regularly to
review student progress, share effective strategies, and adjust instruction
to meet individual needs. The support teams ensure that teachers have
dedicated time to develop effective lessons, differentiate instruction, and
implement interventions that help all students, particularly foster youth,
English learners, and low-income students, succeed.
PLC Support Teams are made of Music and PE teachers, as well as
certificated substitutes.
Elementary PLC support teams will contribute to improvement in our
student outcomes in M2.2 i-Ready ELA, M2.6 Math K-2, and M2.7 Math 3-
6.

actions.title:
Intervention System:
Intensive Reading
Intervention for SWD
actions.description:
The district provides intensive reading intervention, when needed based on
data, for students with disabilities (SWD) to build strong literacy skills and
s u p port their academic success. Trained teachers use evidence-based
programs and personalized instruction to help students improve reading
fluency, comprehension, and confidence. By focusing on reading
intervention, we aim to close the learning gaps for SWD.
Intensive reading intervention will contribute to improvement in our student
outcomes M2.1 ELA and M2.2 i-Ready Reading for SWD.
This action addresses the requirements for LREBG. For 2025-26, LREBG
provides $9,000 to fund this action as a part of Goal 2 Action 2.2,
elementary reading intervention program, which is also LREBG funded.
Research shows that targeting response to interventions can be effective
with an effect size of 1.29 and that targeted interventions for SWD is
effective with an effect size of 0.77 (Hattie, 2018).

goals.description:
All students will have appropriately assigned teachers with access to curriculum-aligned instructional
materials where state academic standards are implemented in safe, clean, and functional school
facilities.

actions.title:
Universal Program:
Textbook and
Instructional
Materials Adoptions
actions.description:
We will continue to make sure that all students have access to the most
current and effective textbooks and educational resources. This action
involves regularly updating and adopting new materials that align with the
l a test educational standards and practices. By providing students with
high-quality, relevant learning tools, we enhance their educational
experience and support effective teaching in every classroom.
Materials adoptions will contribute to our outcomes in metric M3.2
Instructional Materials.

actions.title:
Universal Program:
Visual Arts and
Performing Arts
(VAPA) K-12
actions.description:
We will refine the K-12 Visual and Performing Arts (VAPA) program to
ensure that all students have access to a broad and diverse course of
study in the arts. This initiative supports the development of
comprehensive arts education in music, dance, theater, and visual arts,
enriching students' learning experiences and fostering creativity.
By expanding VAPA opportunities, we provide students with the tools to
explore their artistic talents and express themselves in various forms,
contributing to a well-rounded education and equipping students with
critical skills such as creativity, collaboration, and communication, which
are essential for success in many future careers.
VAPA will contribute to our student outcomes in metric M3.12 VAPA.

actions.title:
Universal Program:
Evaluation of School
Facility Conditions
actions.description:
We will continue to evaluate school facility conditions using the Facilities
Inspection Tool (FIT) to ensure that all school environments are in good
repair, safe, clean, and conducive to learning. Maintaining excellent
physical conditions in our schools is crucial for fostering a healthy learning
atmosphere where students can focus on their studies and thrive
academically.
Annually evaluating our school facility conditions will contribute to our
outcomes in metric M3.3 Facility.
No cost is associated with this action as it is a part of the regular
responsibility of the Business Services department.

actions.title:
Robust Professional
Development:
Implementation of
State Standards
actions.description:
We will continue to provide professional development to teachers and
school administrators to support the continued implementation of state
standards as part of our MTSS Academics. More details are found in Goal
2 Actions 2.16 to 2.26 and 2.44.
Professional development will contribute to many different student
outcomes, and is summarized in metric M3.4 State Standards Professional
Learning.

actions.title:
Targeted Program:
Building Teacher
Leadership
actions.description:
We will continue to build teacher leadership at each school site. The
leadership team will be made up of elementary or secondary chairs. The
chairs will work collaboratively with administrators to facilitate dialogue with
teachers, analyze data, plan, and support implementation of a school plan
and professional development for cultural proficiency and implementation
of state standards.
Having teacher leaders is important to collaboratively build a welcoming,
inclusive school culture and make sure the instructional programs are
aligned to state standards and based on school data. This is important for
the success of all students, and is especially important for foster youth,
English learners, and low-income students who face additional barriers to
learning.
Site leadership teams will contribute to our outcomes in many metrics,
especially M3.4 State Standards Professional Learning and M3.8
Professional Learning.

actions.title:
Targeted Program:
LCAP School Site
Allocation
actions.description:
We will continue to provide each school with an allocation to be used to
increase and improve support for foster youth, English learners, and low-
income students. Each school can use these funds to implement tailored
strategies and programs that directly support the academic and social-
emotional growth of the target students based on their school data. For
example, schools may provide additional professional development on
culturally proficient engagement strategies and trauma-informed practices,
interventions such as extended learning time through after-school
programs, family engagement activities like STEM night, childcare support
for special events, or transportation for college visits.
Action 3.6 is part of our strategy to provide targeted support for schools
that received or has any student groups that received the lowest
performance level on one or more state indicators.
LCAP site allocation will contribute to our student outcomes in many school
metrics, and is summarized in M1.1 Graduation Rate, M1.4 Engagement
and Sense of Belonging, M2.1 ELA, and M2.5 Math. Please see the
school’s Single Plan for School Achievement (SPSA) for more information
and specific metrics.

actions.title:
Targeted Program:
AVID K-12
actions.description:
We will continue to support K-12 AVID (Advancement Via Individual
Determination) program, designed to prepare students for college
readiness and success in a global society. This program provides
strategies and learning opportunities to help students develop critical
thinking, literacy, and math skills. AVID is available to all students, with a
particular focus on foster youth, English learners, and low-income
students.
AVID will contribute to our student outcomes in metrics M3.11 AVID.

actions.title:
Targeted Program:
Career Education
(Previously CTE)
actions.description:
We will continue to provide additional support for Career Education,
formerly known as Career and Technical Education (CTE), to provide
students with practical, hands-on training and learning experiences in
various career fields. This program equips students with the skills and
knowledge necessary for both immediate employment and future
educational opportunities. By integrating academic learning with career-
focused training, we prepare students to successfully enter the workforce
and pursue their career goals with confidence. Career Education is
available for all students, with a particular focus on foster youth, English
learners, and low-income students.
Career Education will contribute to our student outcomes in metric M3.10
CTE.

actions.title:
Targeted Program:
New Teacher
Induction
actions.description:
We will continue to support the New Teacher Induction program. This
program provides essential training and mentoring to help beginning
teachers develop effective teaching strategies and classroom management
skills that are crucial in the first two years of teaching.
We have a higher percentage of our beginning teachers serving students
at Title-1 schools, which means they provide instruction to large portions of
low-income students who face higher barriers to learning. By investing in
the success of our new educators, we ensure that they are well-prepared
to enrich the learning experiences of all students and reduce barriers to
learning for our foster youth, English learners, and low-income students.
Induction will contribute to our outcomes in metric M3.1 Credentialed
Teachers.

actions.title:
Targeted Program:
Technology Licenses
and Subscriptions
actions.description:
We will continue to provide supplemental instructional technology licenses
and subscriptions to support standards-based instruction, assessment and
progress monitoring, and intervention. The licenses and subscriptions will
extend learning as part of supplemental instruction and support our
comprehensive assessment system and data analysis (See Actions 2.8,
2.9, 2.10, and 2.11).
Extending learning and having the appropriate assessment tools as part of
our comprehensive assessment system is important to provide the
information we need to target interventions and support. This is important
for all students, and is especially crucial for our foster youth, English
learners, and low-income students who face additional barriers to learning.
Technology licenses and subscriptions will contribute to many different
student outcomes, but is summarized in metric M3.6 Identify Areas to
Improve Delivering Instruction.

actions.title:
Targeted Program:
Unified VAPA
actions.description:
We will continue to provide a Unified Visual Arts and Performing Arts
(Unified VAPA) program where students from special education and
general education learn and grow together. This inclusive approach
enriches the school community by celebrating all abilities and perspectives.
Unified VAPA will contribute to our metric M3.12 VAPA.
Cost is accounted for using general fund. The estimated 6 sections for
2025-26 is $130,200 at $21,700 per section.

goals.description:
By June 2027, Silverado High School will have a culturally proficient whole child support system to
meet the academic, social-emotional, behavioral, and mental health needs of all students through
actions such as lowering staff to student ratio, ensuring instruction is rigorous and engaging, and
providing additional learning options.

actions.title:
Silverado Summer
School
actions.description:
This action was DISCONTINUED due to budget changes.
We will provide targeted summer school at Silverado High School to
facilitate credit recovery and increase elective options to ensure timely
graduation for students requiring in-person support. This dedicated
program leverages the strong, established relationships between staff and
students to enhance learning effectiveness and engagement.
The available district summer school options include online learning and
summer school located at a different school site. Offering this at Silverado
addresses transportation challenges and avoids the impersonal nature of
online recovery options, reinforcing a supportive educational environment
directly within the community.
Silverado Summer School will contribute to improving our student
outcomes in metrics M4.2 Grad Rate and M4.3 One-Year Grad Rate.

actions.title:
Additional ML ELD
Instructional Aide
Support
actions.description:
We will have additional hours of multilingual ELD instructional aides (IAs)
to support English learners at Silverado High School, ensuring they receive
personalized attention and guidance in their language acquisition journey.
The aides enhance classroom communication and understanding,
providing necessary language support during lessons.
Additional ML IAs will contribute to improving our student outcomes in
metric M4.8 English Learner Progress.
Cost is accounted for using Title-I funds, at an estimated $40,215.00 for
2025-26.

actions.title:
Additional Sections:
Math
actions.description:
We will increase the number of math sections at Silverado High School to
address the high demand and specific needs identified through recent data
analysis. This expansion allows for smaller class sizes and extended
learning opportunities, providing more individualized attention and support
to each student. Additional math sections aim to improve math proficiency
and graduation rate.
Additional math sections will contribute to improving our student outcomes
in metric M4.7 Math.
Cost ($100,000) is included in Action 4.4 using Equity Multiplier Funds.

actions.title:
Additional Sections:
Electives
actions.description:
We will increase elective sections and expand elective course offerings at
Silverado High School to help students meet graduation requirements and
explore varied interests. The increase in elective sections provides more
opportunities for students to engage in learning that aligns with their
passions and future career goals. Addressing the credit shortfall in
electives, this strategy ensures students have the necessary credits to
graduate on time while enriching their educational experience.
Additional electives sections will contribute to improving our student
outcomes in metric M4.5 College/Career, M4.2 Grad Rate, and M4.3 One-
Year Grad Rate.

actions.title:
Intensive
Professional
Development:
Engagement
Strategies
actions.description:
We will have additional, intensive professional development at Silverado
High School focused on rigorous academics and engaging instructional
strategies. The professional development will further equip teachers with
effective strategies to enhance teaching and learning.
In order to see if professional development is increasing evidence-based
engagement and academic strategies in the classroom, the school will
collaboratively develop a classroom strategy monitoring tool that will be
used to monitor implementation. The classroom monitoring tool will be
developed in Fall 2024 and baseline data will be collected and the target
for Year 3 will be established in Spring 2025. The monitoring tool will be
implementation metric M4.9.
Engagement strategies will contribute to improving our student outcomes
in metrics M4.4 Suspension, M4.6 ELA, and M4.7 Math.
Cost ($4,800) is accounted for using Title-IIA funds under action 3.4,
school site allocation.

actions.title:
Intensive
Professional
Development:
Trauma-Informed
Teaching
actions.description:
We will provide additional professional development on trauma-informed
teaching, ensuring educators are well-equipped to support students who
have experienced trauma and disruptions in their lives. This approach
fosters a safe and understanding classroom environment, crucial for
learning at an alternative high school where many of our students have
experienced traumas that result in barriers to learning.
Trauma-informed teaching will contribute to improving our student
outcomes in metrics M4.2 Grad Rate and M4.4 Suspension.
Cost ($4,800) is accounted for using Title-IIA funds under action 3.4,
school site allocation.

actions.title:
Intensive
Professional
Development:
Standards-Based
Instruction in an
Alternative Setting
actions.description:
We will provide ongoing professional development (PD) on delivering
standards-based instruction tailored to alternative classroom settings. The
professional development will equip teachers with strategies to engage
students effectively and meet diverse learning needs where classrooms
may have students on many different learning pathways and with very
different academic needs. The PD will include small group strategies,
universal design for learning strategies, differentiated instruction, and
blended learning.
The PD will contribute to improving our student outcomes in metrics M4.2
Grad Rat, M4.6 ELA and M4.7 Math.
Cost ($4,800) is accounted for using Title-IIA funds under action 3.4,
school site allocation.

actions.title:
Intensive
Professional
Development:
Integrated Language
Support
actions.description:
We will provide professional development on integrated language support
across curricular areas, tailored to alternative classroom settings. The
professional development will equip teachers with language support
strategies to engage students effectively and meet different language
learning needs where classrooms may have students on many different
learning pathways and with very different language needs.
Integrated language support will contribute to improvement in our student
outcomes in metric M4.8 ELPI.
Cost ($4,800) is accounted for using Title-IIA funds under action 3.4,
school site allocation.

actions.title:
Additional Mental
Health Counseling
actions.description:
We will provide additional mental health counseling to provide more
personalized support for Silverado students as they face additional barriers
to social-emotional and behavioral well-being. Enhanced mental health
counseling resources help students navigate their educational path and
improve overall school climate, contributing to higher engagement and
success rates.
Additional counseling will contribute to improvement in our student
outcomes in metric M4.4 Suspension.

actions.title:
College/Career
Experiences
actions.description:
We will provide students with firsthand experiences in higher education
and career fields by organizing visits to colleges and trade schools, and
meeting with industry experts. These opportunities help students explore
various career paths, understand educational requirements, and gain
insights from professionals in the industry.
College/career experiences support informed decision-making for students'
future educational and career choices and will contribute to improvement in
our student outcomes in metric M4.5 College/Career.
Cost is accounted for using site-based allocation for Title-I funds, an
estimated $5,000.
